Ad Text:
We are partnering with a large healthcare organisation in Melbourne that is currently running a number of high-impact proof of concepts focused on automation, process improvement, and AI-driven solutions.

They are seeking an experienced Automation Developer / Analyst Programmer who can engage directly with stakeholders, translate requirements into technical solutions, and rapidly deliver working outcomes.

This is a hands-on role suited to someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment and has previously delivered automation and integration solutions end-to-end.

Key Responsibilities

Work closely with business stakeholders to gather and refine requirements
Design and develop automation solutions across Microsoft platforms
Build and optimise workflows using Power Automate
Develop reporting and dashboards using Power BI
Create and manage SharePoint-based solutions, including SharePoint Lists
Develop and configure AI agents using Copilot Studio
Integrate systems and services via APIs
Ensure solutions align with security and identity standards (Entra ID / Azure AD)
Deliver proof of concepts and iterate rapidly based on feedback
Provide technical guidance and support to users throughout delivery
Required Skills and Experience

Strong background in software engineering or development
Proven experience delivering automation solutions in enterprise environments
Hands-on expertise with Power Automate and Power BI
Experience working with SharePoint Lists and SharePoint-based solutions
Exposure to Copilot Studio and/or AI-driven agent development
Strong experience with API integration and connecting disparate systems
Solid understanding of Microsoft identity platforms, including Entra ID (Azure AD)
Ability to work across both technical and business domains
Strong communication skills and stakeholder engagement capability
Desirable

Experience within healthcare or large, complex organisations
Exposure to rapid prototyping or proof-of-concept environments
Familiarity with Microsoft Azure ecosystem more broadly
What’s on Offer

Immediate start on a high-impact project
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge automation and AI initiatives
Strong likelihood of contract extension or longer-term engagement
Collaborative and forward-thinking environment

Location: Mawson Lakes, SA (preferred)
Various locations considered | Telework available

Own and lead enterprise systems architecture and backend configuration for sustainment applications, with a strong focus on Microsoft Dynamics ERP. This is a hands-on technical leadership role, driving integration, automation and digital transformation across complex environments.

You will remain technically engaged while leading a small specialist team delivering enterprise sustainment systems that meet business and contractual requirements.

 

What You’ll Do

Lead systems architecture for sustainment software and ERP platforms

Own Microsoft Dynamics backend configuration, customisation and integration

Solve complex integration and process issues across enterprise systems

Deliver sustainment applications using Agile and DevSecOps (CI/CD) practices

Execute the Digital Transformation Roadmap for sustainment tools

Integrate ERP systems with Enterprise IT and engineering platforms

Provide technical leadership and mentoring across architecture and DevSecOps

 

About You

You are a senior systems architect or enterprise applications lead with strong experience in ERP systems, Microsoft Dynamics, backend configuration and system integration. You enjoy solving complex technical problems and staying hands-on while leading delivery.

Essential:

Strong experience in systems architecture and enterprise application integration

Hands-on experience configuring Microsoft Dynamics

On-premises installation of Business Central (BC), Customer Engagement (CE), and ERP systems

Development experience and strong understanding of SDLC best practices

Delivery experience using Agile and DevSecOps (CI/CD)

Strong background in software configuration management and application lifecycle management.

Clear, confident communication with technical and business stakeholders

Desirable:

Experience in Moodle and VetTrak

Experience in PsiGen PsiCapture (OCR, Document Automation, Process Automation)

Dashboarding and reporting experience

Jira / Confluence (Atlassian)

AWS or cloud platform experience

We’re partnering with a leading Australian brand undergoing a significant transformation across Data & AI. This program is focused on rethinking how contract data is understood and how procurement experiences are designed. 
  
This is not a strategy or architecture-only role. This is about building real, working products. You’ll sit inside a high-impact transformation team, developing internal POCs that layer on top of established enterprise platforms - leveraging modern AI tooling, data platforms, and cloud infrastructure. The work spans contract ingestion, document intelligence, AI agents, and user-facing tools. 
  
We’re looking for someone who can move seamlessly from concept to code - comfortable whiteboarding with architects one moment, then building and deploying working solutions the next. 

Location: Sydney (Hybrid | 3 days/week in office)
Length: 12 months (possible extensions)
Rate: $1000-$1100 + GST

What You’ll Do

Build and iterate end-to-end AI-powered POCs — from ingestion and extraction through to analysis and front-end experience
Develop solutions across structured and unstructured data, including document processing and intelligent workflows
Design and implement AI-driven components (LLMs, agents, automation workflows) embedded in real applications
Rapidly prototype, test, and refine solutions, demonstrating progress to business and technical stakeholders
Collaborate with enterprise architecture and platform teams to ensure alignment with broader technology strategy
Contribute to, and challenge technical design decisions early in the lifecycle
Translate ambiguous problem spaces into clear, executable build plans
Produce clear documentation to enable handover to internal or scaled delivery teams for productionisation
What You’ll Bring

Proven experience building and deploying AI-enabled applications, not just experimenting in notebooks
Hands-on delivery experience with AWS Bedrock in a real-world setting
Strong capability working across data pipelines.
Ability to develop functional front-end interfaces (React) for internal tools and POCs
Experience integrating LLMs, document intelligence, and/or agent-based systems into usable applications
Strong communication skills and able to engage both non-technical stakeholders and enterprise architecture teams
A self-directed mindset - i.e. comfortable operating in ambiguity and defining your own path to delivery
A bias toward action - you build, test, and iterate quickly
Experience with modern data and AI platforms (e.g. Snowflake, Databricks, Fabric, Redshift) is highly regarded
If you have any questions about this role or others we have available you can email our team directly via jack@thedrivegroup.com.au
